# Generated by Claude Toad — Review before use

# claude-toad

CLI tool that scans repos and generates complete, project-specific Claude Code configurations.

## Tech Stack

- TypeScript 5.9.3 (strict mode)
- Node.js CLI (npx executable)
- Vitest for testing
- GitHub Actions CI
- Anthropic API for generation
- Smidge API for skill generation from media

## Architecture

- `src/` — CLI source code
- `dist/` — compiled output
- `tests/` — Vitest test suite
- Four core commands: `init`, `new`, `package`, `add-skill`
- Two-phase pipeline: scan (pure filesystem analysis) → generate (AI-powered via Anthropic API)
- API keys stored locally at `~/.claude-toad/config.json`
- No database, no ORM — file system and API only

## Key Commands

- `npm test` — run Vitest test suite
- `npm run lint` — lint the codebase
- `npm run build` — compile TypeScript to dist/
- `npx claude-toad init` — scan repo and generate .claude/ config
- `npx claude-toad new` — scaffold new project with .claude/ baked in
- `npx claude-toad package` — bundle .claude/ for team distribution
- `npx claude-toad add-skill` — generate skills from URLs, PDFs, videos

## Conventions

- Strict TypeScript everywhere — no `any`, no implicit returns
- All functions must have explicit type annotations
- Use `interface` for object shapes, `type` for unions/intersections
- Prefer `async/await` over raw promises
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never swallow exceptions silently
- File naming: kebab-case for all source files
- Imports: use explicit named imports, no wildcard `*` imports
- No runtime dependencies on frameworks — this is a standalone CLI
- Keep generated CLAUDE.md output under 150 lines per Anthropic guidance
- Pure functions for scanning/analysis; side effects isolated to I/O boundaries
- Tests colocated in `tests/` directory, mirroring `src/` structure
- Test files named `*.test.ts`

## Gotchas

- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env var is auto-detected; falls back to `~/.claude-toad/config.json`
- Smidge API key is separate from Anthropic key — prompted on first `add-skill` use
- The scan phase uses zero AI — it's pure file system reads; only generation calls the API
- `dist/` is committed output — ensure it stays in sync after source changes
- TypeScript 5.9.3 is bleeding edge — some editor plugins may lag behind language features
- Generated configs target the consuming project's stack, not claude-toad's own stack
- `package` command produces a .zip — not an npm package, not a tarball
